rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (expired), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for the web, browser extension, Android, and iOS." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting; Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections. Residual uncertainty: Dossier completeness not confirmed for this treasury proposal. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 20.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional voting is blocked until dossier quality gates pass.
top_fixes: Provide: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Provide: Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Provide: Missing dossier section: complete proposal summary
missing_evidence: Deep research dossier is required for treasury actions before directional voting.
Complete proposal summary, budget analysis, feasibility, risks, alternatives, and failure-mode sections.
Missing dossier section: complete proposal summary
Missing dossier section: community impact analysis

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Deliverables: Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for the web, browser extension, Android, and iOS., Users rely on Eternl for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction., This proposal enables 12 months of operations, maintenance, and improvements., It includes frontend and backend maintenance and development, backend infrastructure, user support, and day-to-day operations.[^1] The goal is to keep Eternl secure, compatible, stable, and available as Cardano keeps evolving.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for the web, browser extension, Android, and iOS.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Users rely on Eternl for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): This proposal enables 12 months of operations, maintenance, and improvements.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): It includes frontend and backend maintenance and development, backend infrastructure, user support, and day-to-day operations.[^1] The goal is to keep Eternl secure, compatible, stable, and available as Cardano keeps evolving.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Budget and Budget Breakdown The Path to Sustainability Eternl will introduce a Pro plan for personal and company use.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): | Plan | Price | |----------|--------------------------:| | Personal | $96/year ($8/month) | | Company | $384/year ($32/month)[^2] | Eternl has an install base of about 100,000 on the browser extension and about 30,000 across Android and iOS.
missing: Independent evidence for: Budget and Budget Breakdown The Path to Sustainability Eternl will introduce a Pro plan for personal and company use.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for the web, browser extension, Android, and iOS." though 7 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Budget and Budget Breakdown The Path to Sustainability Eternl will introduce a Pro plan for personal and company use." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
Gate disbursement on milestones that must be met before money moves.
Provide a clawback or refund path if the work is not delivered.
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
delivery controls, milestones, assurance, or remedy path

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
